title: That's So True
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I could go and read your mind
  > [#1, 2 votes] Gracie Abrams explained the meaning behind these lines in the story of my song: Feeling like you know someone so well, that no words are required to understand exactly what it is the’re thinking
Think about your dumb face all the time
  > [#2, 3 votes] Gracie Abrams explained the meaning behind these lines in the story of my song: I think you’re dumb, but you’re in my head constantly. Sort off like this push and pull between like, I’m so over it,...
Living in your glass house, I'm outside, uh
  > [#3, 29 votes] Abrams' assertion that the subject of this song lives in a “glass house” can be interpreted in a few ways. The well-known metaphor “those who live in glass houses shouldn’t throw stones,” can be...
Looking in her big blue eyes
  > [#4, 5 votes] In Story of My Song, Gracie discussed this line: This piece felt important to Audrey and I. And I remember us, between sips of wine, kind of listing all of the potential qualities that we could...
Did it just to hurt me, make me cry
  > [#5, 3 votes] Gracie Abrams explained the meaning behind these lines in the story of my song: I also feel like this song is a good example of being like broken up with can sort of trigger some pretty navel gazing,...
Smiling through it all, yeah, that's my life
  > [#6, 33 votes] A riff on the “Can’t Believe This My Life” / LeBron James Smiling meme: As explained during her Interscope Records artist showcase, the meme holds significance for Gracie and her best friend turned...

[Pre-Chorus]
You're an idiot, now I'm sure
Now I'm positive, I should go and warn her

[Chorus]
Ooh, bet you're thinking, "She's so cool"
  > [#7, 48 votes] On The Secret of Us (Deluxe), “That’s So True” is preceded by “Cool”, which this line is likely a reference to. “Cool” also has a line in the first verse about kicking back on a couch: You kick back...
Kicking back on your couch, making eyes from across the room
Wait, I think I've been there too, ooh

[Verse 2]
What'd she do to get you off? (Uh-huh)
Taking down her hair like, oh, my God
  > [#8, 3 votes] In Story of My Song, Gracie shared her love of this verse and this line in particular: Similarly to the blue eyes piece, I had recently cut my hair off, so I felt like that’s another thing that we...
Taking off your shirt, I did that once
Or twice, uh
No, I know, I know I'll fuck off (Uh-huh)
But I think I like her, she's so fun
  > [#9, 49 votes] In Gracie’s 2021 song “Rockland”, the first verse ends with: I’m sure that I would like her If I were slightly nicer Which these lines are likely a reference to.
Wait, I think I hate her, I'm not that evolved, I'm sorry

[Pre-Chorus]
She's missing it, sad, sad boy
Not my business, but I had to warn ya

[Chorus]
Ooh, bet you're thinking, "She's so cool"
  > [#7, see above]
Kicking back on your couch, making eyes from across the room
Wait, I think I've been there too, ooh
Ooh, you've got me thinking, "She's so cool"
But I know what I know and you're just another dude
Ooh, that's so true, ooh

[Bridge]
Made it out alive, but I think I lost it
Said that I was fine, said it from my coffin
Remember how I died when you started walking?
That's my life, that's my life
I'll put up a fight, taking out my earrings
Don't you know the vibe? Don't you know the feeling?
And she'll spend the night, catch me on your ceiling
That's your price, that's your price
Well

[Chorus]
Mm, bet you're thinking, "She's so cool"
  > [#7, see above]
Kicking back on your couch, making eyes from across the room
Wait, I think I've been there too, ooh
Ooh, you've got me thinking, "She's so cool"
But I know what I know, and you're just another dude
Ooh, that's so true, ooh-ooh, oh
